name: Chengdu
lifestyle: Relaxed tier-1.5 city with one of the lowest costs of living among Chinese metros and an outsized food culture. English usage is limited and the pace is slower than Shanghai or Beijing, but premium housing and international school tuition run well below tier-1 rates. Z visa or talent residency is the typical entry.

true_fat_fire.description: Jinjiang 3BR high-rise or Lan Kwai Fong luxury apartment, full-time ayi plus driver. Hotpot crawls, private dining, teahouses, 2-3 international trips annually.

guide.groceries: Imported grocers plus premium local produce and seafood, with high-quality pantry goods, wine, and gift-grade fruit bought without much restraint.
guide.dining: Hotpot, modern Sichuan, and hotel dining show up often, with nicer nights commonly around RMB500-1,200 per person.
guide.rhythm: Mostly driver or ride-hail, premium wellness and beauty, and a lifestyle that treats boutique hotels and Chengdu-Chongqing leisure as normal.

comfortable_expat.description: Tongzilin/High-Tech Zone 2BR modern apartment, local dining nightly (cheap and excellent), e-bike plus DiDi, monthly regional weekends plus annual long-haul.

guide.groceries: Comfortable city shopping with Sam's-style bulk runs, imported staples, and strong local produce and meat value for home cooking.
guide.dining: Restaurant meals a few times a week plus an occasional nicer dinner, often RMB80-180 casually and RMB300-700 when stepping up.
guide.rhythm: Mostly DiDi and metro, moderate personal-care spending, light housekeeping help, and domestic city breaks rather than constant luxury escapes.

luxury_family.description: High-Tech Zone villa or premium compound near QSI/LEA, full-time ayi plus part-time driver, top international school access when dependents apply, annual home-country trip.

guide.groceries: Large family basket with imported snacks, proteins, fruit, and household staples, supported by easy delivery and bulk-shopping top-ups.
guide.dining: Family-friendly dining most weekends plus adult meals out, with casual family meals often RMB100-220 per person and destination dinners materially higher.
guide.rhythm: Ayi plus some driver coverage, child activity and school logistics, and an annual rhythm built around school calendars and longer trips.
